op.name: polymul
op.notes: Multiply polynomials. Cost: n1 * n2 FLOPs.
op.summary: Find the product of two polynomials.

blocks.text: Find the product of two polynomials.

blocks.name: note
blocks.body:
This forms part of the old polynomial API. Since version 1.4, the
   new polynomial API defined in `numpy.polynomial` is preferred.
   A summary of the differences can be found in the
   :doc:`transition guide </reference/routines.polynomials>`.
blocks.raw:
.. note::
   This forms part of the old polynomial API. Since version 1.4, the
   new polynomial API defined in `numpy.polynomial` is preferred.
   A summary of the differences can be found in the
   :doc:`transition guide </reference/routines.polynomials>`.

blocks.text:
Finds the polynomial resulting from the multiplication of the two input
polynomials. Each input must be either a poly1d object or a 1D sequence
of polynomial coefficients, from highest to lowest degree.

desc_blocks.text:
The polynomial resulting from the multiplication of the inputs. If
either inputs is a poly1d object, then the output is also a poly1d
object. Otherwise, it is a 1D array of polynomial coefficients from
highest to lowest degree.

blocks.code:
>>> import numpy as np
>>> np.polymul([1, 2, 3], [9, 5, 1])
array([ 9, 23, 38, 17,  3])

blocks.text: poly, polyadd, polyder, polydiv, polyfit, polyint, polysub, polyval
convolve — Array convolution. Same output as polymul, but has parameter for overlap mode.
